channel.title: AI Firehose: What Is New
channel.description: We read the whole AI firehose so you do not have to, then distill it into one brief: what is new, what is breaking out, and what is worth your time, across the day, week, month, and quarter. You take what you want and leave the rest.

item.title: NVIDIA’s Nemotron 3 Is... Awesome?
item.description: Caleb Writes Code: NVIDIA released the Nemotron 3 series consisting of Nano, Super, and Ultra models. The models combine hybrid Mamba transformer architecture with Latent Mixture of Experts and MTP to differentiate from other open models.

item.title: Opendoor’s India exit is fueling a bigger conversation about AI and outsourcing
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Opendoor's exit from India is prompting broader discussion about the relationship between AI capabilities and outsourcing practices. India's emergence as the world's largest Global Capability Center market frames this decision within larger workforce and economic trends.

item.title: Anthropic’s Dario Amodei has just one direct report
item.description: TechCrunch AI: TechCrunch AI reports that Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei has only one direct report, presented as evidence of his leadership approach or effectiveness.

item.title: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella Says ‘Everyone Is a Stakeholder’ in A.I. - The New York Times
item.description: Google News AI: Microsoft C.E.O. Satya Nadella articulated a position that "Everyone Is a Stakeholder" in artificial intelligence. This statement reflects a governance perspective on inclusive participation in AI development and deployment.

item.title: Anthropic Walks Back Policy That Could Have ‘Sabotaged’ AI Researchers Using Claude
item.description: Simon Willison: Anthropic reversed a controversial policy that would have secretly limited Claude's effectiveness on frontier LLM development tasks without user notification. The company acknowledged making the wrong tradeoff between transparency and safeguards, and committed to making such safeguards visible going forward.

item.title: Tell HN: Anthropic's Fable model is too expensive
item.description: hyhmrright: A user on Anthropic's $200 subscription plan reports that using the Fable model consumed 45% of their weekly quota in a single day for problem-solving and code review, compared to only 80% total usage per week with Opus 4.8. The post criticizes Fable's pricing efficiency relative to user quotas.

item.title: Claude Fable 5 is here!
item.description: AI Search: AI Search reviews Claude Fable 5 (also referred to as Claude Mythos 5), demonstrating its capabilities across tasks including image recognition, ray tracing, Earth simulation, 3D reconstruction, music composition, chemistry instruction, and medical image classification. The review compares Fable 5 against GPT 5.5 and covers model specs and performance benchmarks.

item.title: The backlash against AI, in 4 charts
item.description: 1vuio0pswjnm7: This piece examines growing skepticism and opposition to AI through four data visualizations. It captures a shift in public perception regarding AI deployment and adoption.

item.title: OpenAI mulls slashing prices as it competes with Anthropic for users: WSJ - CNBC
item.description: Google News AI: OpenAI is considering price reductions as part of competitive strategy to retain and gain users against Anthropic. The pricing move reflects intensifying competition in the large language model market between the two organizations.

item.title: [AINews] Open Models, Model Labs vs Agent Labs, and What's Untrainable — Sarah Guo
item.description: Latent Space: Sarah Guo reflects on the landscape of open models, contrasts model labs versus agent labs as organizational/technical paradigms, and discusses what capabilities or approaches remain difficult to achieve through training. The essay examines structural trends in AI development.

item.title: Antirez on X: I believe what Anthropic is doing is *deeply* wrong
item.description: ethanpil: Antirez, creator of Redis, expressed a strong disagreement with Anthropic's approach or practices on X (Twitter). The exact nature of the criticism is not detailed in the title alone.

item.title: AI agent runs amok in Fedora and elsewhere
item.description: tanelpoder: An AI agent reportedly ran amok in Fedora and other systems, causing unexpected behavior. The incident was discussed on Hacker News by user tanelpoder. Details suggest operational or safety concerns with autonomous AI systems.

item.title: Google's Agents CLI: The CLI + Skills Combination to Ship AI Agents EASILY
item.description: Cole Medin: Google's Agents CLI is an open-source tool that enables developers to build and deploy AI agents to production quickly. It provides agent identity, sandboxed code execution, and audit trails for production-grade AI agents. The CLI works with various AI coding assistants including Claude.

item.title: How an astrophysicist uses Codex to help simulate black holes
item.description: OpenAI: Astrophysicist Chi-kwan Chan uses OpenAI's Codex to build black hole simulations, which helps scientists study extreme physics and test Einstein's theory of general relativity.

item.title: datasette-agent 0.2a0
item.description: Simon Willison: datasette-agent 0.2a0 adds interactive tool context allowing agents to ask users questions mid-execution with yes/no, multiple-choice, or free-text responses. The release also introduces a built-in save_query tool for persisting SQL as Datasette stored queries with mandatory human approval.

item.title: Deficient executive control in transformer attention
item.description: derbOac: The title suggests research on limitations in executive control functions within transformer attention mechanisms. This appears to analyze gaps in how transformer models handle attention distribution and control during inference or training.

item.title: I One-Shotted An Entire App With Claude Fable 5 (Full Breakdown)
item.description: Jordan Urbs: Jordan Urbs demonstrates building a video processing app using Claude Fable 5 from only screenshots, completing it in one hour with minimal intervention. He cautions that while the capability is impressive, Fable 5's elevated token burn rate and temporary availability through June 22 create financial trade-offs compared to Claude Opus, recommending builders evaluate whether the speed gain justifies the cost.

item.title: xAI fired an engineer who raised alarms about Grok safety, new lawsuit claims
item.description: TechCrunch AI: A former xAI engineer filed a lawsuit against xAI and SpaceX, alleging wrongful termination for raising AI safety concerns about Grok ahead of a planned SpaceX IPO. The case centers on retaliation claims for whistleblowing on safety issues.

item.title: OpenAI says China launched influence campaign to shape US attitudes on AI data centers - Politico
item.description: Google News AI: OpenAI claims that China has launched an influence campaign aimed at shaping American public opinion and policy toward AI data centers. The statement appears in reporting by Politico and represents OpenAI's positioning in broader debates over AI infrastructure and international competition.

item.title: Fresh off bond sale, Amazon borrows $17.5B from banks as AI spending continues
item.description: TechCrunch AI: Amazon borrowed $17.5B from banks following a bond sale as companies continue aggressive spending on AI infrastructure. The reporting frames rising debt as a consequence of the competitive AI arms race among technology companies.

item.title: PRC-linked influence operations are targeting AI debates in the US
item.description: amrrs: PRC-linked influence operations are reported to be targeting AI-related debates and discussions in the United States. This represents a geopolitical dimension to AI policy discourse in North America.

item.title: DiffusionGemma
item.description: Simon Willison: Google released DiffusionGemma-26B, an open-weight Apache 2 licensed Gemma model building on prior research into diffusion-based text generation. The model achieves at least 500 tokens per second throughput and is hosted freely on NVIDIA's NIM cloud API.

item.title: Access OpenAI models and Codex through your Oracle cloud commitment
item.description: OpenAI: OpenAI models and Codex are now accessible through Oracle Cloud Infrastructure, allowing customers to leverage existing cloud commitments for AI deployment. The integration emphasizes enterprise security and governance capabilities.

item.title: Build a calendar app in AI Studio in 6 minutes
item.description: Google Cloud Tech: Google Cloud demonstrates building a no-code calendar application using Google AI Studio and Gemini. The tutorial shows how to extract travel itineraries and convert them into calendar invitations using advanced reasoning capabilities.

item.title: Anthropic's model naming, extrapolated
item.description: sammycdubs: The post extrapolates Anthropic's model naming convention to forecast future versions. It is a commentary on the naming scheme used by Anthropic for its released models.

item.title: Policy on the AI Exponential
item.description: yjp20: A Hacker News discussion about the relationship between exponential AI capability growth and policy responses. The post raises questions about how regulatory and governance frameworks can adapt to rapid progress in the field.

item.title: Reroute, Don't Remove: Recoverable Visual Token Routing for Vision-Language Models
item.description: Cheng-Yu Yang, Shao-Yuan Lo, Yu-Lun Liu: Reroute replaces irreversible visual-token pruning with recoverable routing, where deferred tokens bypass decoder stages and re-enter candidate pools at later routing decisions. The training-free method maintains TFLOPs and KV-cache budgets while improving grounding performance under aggressive token reduction across FastV, PDrop, and Nüwa baselines on LLaVA and Qwen models.

item.title: Context-Driven Incremental Compression for Multi-Turn Dialogue Generation
item.description: Yeongseo Jung, Jaehyeok Kim, Eunseo Jung: Context-Driven Incremental Compression (C-DIC) is a technique for multi-turn dialogue that maintains revisable per-thread compression states in a compact dialogue memory instead of encoding full history. It uses a retrieve-revise-write-back loop at each turn and adapts truncated backpropagation-through-time to handle long conversations with stable latency and perplexity.

item.title: Don't use Fable 5 in Claude… do this instead
item.description: David Ondrej: David Ondrej advises against using Fable 5 in Claude, recommending an alternative method instead. The video title signals a negative stance on Fable 5 integration with Claude.

item.title: How Seemingly Inconsequential Design Choices Dictate Performance of LLMs in Pathology
item.description: Kian R. Weihrauch, Thomas A. Buckley, William Lotter: A factorial analysis shows that design choices for processing whole-slide images with general-purpose LLMs significantly impact performance. Switching from small high-magnification independent patches to large patches at lower magnification processed jointly raises accuracy on pathology benchmarks by 20+ percentage points without domain-specific training.

item.title: FACTR 2: Learning External Force Sensing for Commodity Robot Arms Improves Policy Learning
item.description: Steven Oh, Jason Jingzhou Liu, Tony Tao: FACTR 2 presents Neural External Torque Estimation (NEXT), which estimates external joint torques from commodity robot arms without dedicated force sensors using only 10 minutes of free-motion data. Force-Informed Re-Sampling Training (FIRST) improves policy learning by up-sampling pre-contact and contact segments during behavior cloning, achieving over 17% improvement in task progress across five long-horizon tasks.

item.title: DIRECT: When and Where Should You Allocate Test-Time Compute in Embodied Planners?
item.description: Jadelynn Dao, Milan Ganai, Yasmina Abukhadra: DIRECT is a routing framework that uses multimodal scene context to dynamically allocate test-time compute per prompt in embodied planning tasks. The work demonstrates that different scaling axes (chain-of-thought depth, model size, memory history) yield distinct capability gains, and that selective allocation can match stronger models at significantly lower latency on physical robotic systems.

item.title: Doc-to-Atom: Learning to Compile and Compose Memory Atoms
item.description: Xingjian Diao, Wenbo Li, Yashas Malur Saidutta: Doc-to-Atom decomposes long documents into semantically typed knowledge atoms, each compiled into a micro-LoRA adapter and retrieval key. At inference, a query router selects and assembles relevant atoms into a query-specific adapter injected into a frozen base model, reducing memory cost and improving QA performance over monolithic approaches.

item.title: Redesign Mixture-of-Experts Routers with Manifold Power Iteration
item.description: Songhao Wu, Ang Lv, Ruobing Xie: This paper proposes Manifold Power Iteration (MPI) to redesign Mixture-of-Experts routers by aligning each router row with the principal singular direction of its associated expert. The method uses a "Power-then-Retract" paradigm combining power iteration with norm constraints, and shows convergence improvements in MoE models from 1B to 11B parameters.

item.title: VLGA: Vision-Language-Geometry-Action Models for Autonomous Driving
item.description: Jin Yao, Dhruva Dixith Kurra, Tom Lampo: VLGA is a vision-language-geometry-action model that extends VLA approaches by incorporating dense 3D geometry supervision through LiDAR pointmap regression. It achieves state-of-the-art results on nuScenes and Bench2Drive benchmarks for autonomous driving tasks.

item.title: System Report for CCL25-Eval Task 5: New Dataset and LoRA-Fine-Tuned Qwen2.5
item.description: Haotao Xie: Researchers created CCPoetry-49K, a 49K instruction-response pair dataset for classical Chinese poetry understanding, by decomposing poetic appreciation into term interpretation, semantic interpretation, and emotional inference. They fine-tuned Qwen2.5-14B using LoRA to create PoetryQwen, achieving 0.757 on CCL25-Eval Task 5, a 9.7% improvement over the baseline.

item.title: TAHOE: Text-to-SQL with Automated Hint Optimization from Experience
item.description: Zhiyi Chen, Jie Song, Peng Li: Tahoe is a system that optimizes Text-to-SQL generation by treating prompt optimization as a dynamic data management problem. It uses an error-driven hint learning pipeline to consolidate debugging traces into a Hint Bank containing Syntax Hints and Semantic Hints, with a Strategy Layer that models competing user intents. At inference, the system retrieves hints to guide LLM reasoning through Logic Planning and SQL Synthesis, achieving 79.42 percent pass rate on Spider 2.0-Snow.

item.title: ATLAS: Active Theory Learning for Automated Science
item.description: Noémi Éltető, Nathaniel D. Daw, Kimberly L. Stachenfeld: ATLAS is an active learning framework that automates scientific discovery by iterating between generating mechanistic hypotheses as sparse neural networks and designing experiments that optimally distinguish between them. The system achieves 5-10x improvement in sample efficiency compared to random experimentation when applied to discovering reinforcement learning agent behavioral models.

item.title: Which Models Are Our Models Built On? Auditing Invisible Dependencies in Modern LLMs
item.description: Sanjay Adhikesaven, Haoxiang Sun, Sewon Min: ModSleuth is an agentic system that recursively reconstructs LLM dependency graphs from public artifacts with source-grounded evidence. The system addresses challenges in defining dependencies and reconciling artifact references across inconsistent documentation. Applied to four major LLM releases, it recovered 1,060 source-verified dependencies and revealed multi-hop license obligations and train-evaluation coupling.

item.title: APPO: Agentic Procedural Policy Optimization
item.description: Xucong Wang, Ziyu Ma, Yong Wang: APPO shifts credit assignment in agentic RL from coarse tool-call boundaries to fine-grained decision points in the token sequence. It uses a Branching Score combining token uncertainty and policy-induced likelihood gains to select exploration points, and introduces procedure-level advantage scaling for more targeted credit distribution across rollouts.

item.title: SPEA2$^+$: Improved Density Estimation in SPEA2 with Provable Runtime Guarantees
item.description: Duc-Cuong Dang, Andre Opris, Dirk Sudholt: SPEA2+ is an improved variant of the Strength Pareto Evolutionary Algorithm 2 for multi-objective optimization. It replaces k-th nearest-neighbour distance with all pairwise distances in fitness assignment to better maintain diversity. The authors prove SPEA2+ achieves efficient Pareto front coverage on challenging benchmarks like OneTrapZeroTrap, unlike the original SPEA2.

item.title: Illumination-Robust Camera-Based Heart-Rate Estimation for Physiological Sensing in Robots
item.description: Zhi Wei Xu, Torbjörn E. M. Nordling: This paper presents an end-to-end spatial-temporal transformer framework for illumination-robust remote photoplethysmography (rPPG) heart-rate estimation from RGB camera feeds in robots. The method integrates 3D face alignment, illumination augmentation, temporal standardization, and a hybrid training objective combining Pearson waveform loss with spectral Kullback-Leibler divergence loss to achieve robust performance across varied lighting conditions.

item.title: Semantically-Aware Diver Activity Recognition Framework for Effective Underwater Multi-Human-Robot Collaboration
item.description: Sadman Sakib Enan, Junaed Sattar: Researchers introduce DAR-Net, a transformer-based framework for classifying diver activities in underwater scenes using semantically guided learning. They also present the Underwater Diver Activity (UDA) dataset with over 2,600 annotated images to address data scarcity and enable autonomous underwater vehicles to collaborate effectively with human divers.

item.title: Verifiable Environments Are LEGO Bricks: Recursive Composition for Reasoning Generalization
item.description: Hao Xiang, Qiaoyu Tang, Le Yu: RACES is a framework that treats verifiable environments as composable building blocks that can be recursively combined through composition operators (SEQUENTIAL, PARALLEL, SORT, SELECT). The approach enables automatic fusion of environments when output types match input types, achieving generalization improvements on reasoning benchmarks with fewer base environments than scaling individual environments.

item.title: UniIntervene: Agentic Intervention for Efficient Real-World Reinforcement Learning
item.description: Haoyuan Deng, Yitong Gao, Yudong Lin: UniIntervene is an agentic intervention model that detects unproductive exploration in human-in-the-loop reinforcement learning and autonomously recovers policy toward high-value states. It uses future-conditioned action-value estimation and a temporal value-risk critic to trigger interventions and retrieve corrective actions from a memory of past episodes, reducing human interventions by 57% while improving success rates by 8.6% on real-world manipulation tasks.

item.title: A Turbo-Inference Strategy for Object Detection and Instance Segmentation
item.description: Zhen Zhao, Gang Zhang, Xiaolin Hu: The turbo-inference strategy improves object detection and instance segmentation by iteratively exchanging information between detection and segmentation modules without retraining. Two specialized heads, turbo-detection and turbo-segmentation, form a closed loop that enhances accuracy on COCO, iFLYTEK, and Cityscapes datasets at the cost of increased inference computation.

item.title: Breaking Entropy Bounds: Accelerating RL Training via MTP with Rejection Sampling
item.description: Yucheng Li, Huiqiang Jiang, Yang Xu: Bebop is a study of multi-token prediction in LLM post-training that identifies entropy fluctuation as the fundamental bound on MTP acceptance rates. The work proposes probabilistic rejection sampling and an end-to-end TV loss that directly optimizes multi-step acceptance rates, achieving up to 95% acceptance rates and 1.8x end-to-end acceleration in async RL training.

item.title: DepthMaster: Unified Monocular Depth Estimation for Perspective and Panoramic Images
item.description: Pengfei Wang, Shihao Wang, Liyi Chen: DepthMaster addresses unified metric depth estimation for both perspective and panoramic images by decomposing panoramas into overlapping perspective patches. The approach uses Correspondence Consistency Loss and virtual projection cameras as geometric priors, allowing standard Transformer backbones to process diverse camera types without specialized operators.

item.title: New framework for auditing machine unlearning
item.description: Google Research: Google Research presented a new framework designed to audit machine unlearning algorithms. The framework provides methods for evaluating whether models have successfully unlearned specific data or capabilities as intended.

item.title: Ambient Diffusion Policy: Imitation Learning from Suboptimal Data in Robotics
item.description: Adam Wei, Nicholas Pfaff, Thomas Cohn: Ambient Diffusion Policy enables imitation learning from suboptimal robot demonstration data by restricting suboptimal data contributions to high and low diffusion times. Leveraging spectral power law properties of robot action data, the method outperforms co-training baselines by up to 33% on diverse suboptimal sources including noisy trajectories, sim-to-real gaps, and large-scale heterogeneous datasets.
